title: --trace-fst causes internal error on unpacked array with shifted index assignment
body:
When using `--trace-fst`, Verilator hits an internal error if you assign to an unpacked array with shifted indices. Without `--trace-fst`, everything works fine. 

## Code Snippet

```systemverilog
module Test #(
  localparam int DIM1 = 4,
  localparam int DIM2 = 8,
  localparam int SHIFT = 2
) (
  input logic [DIM1-1:0][DIM2-1:0] in
);

  logic [DIM1-1:0][DIM2+SHIFT-1:SHIFT] internal;

  for (genvar dim1 = 0; dim1 < DIM1; dim1++) begin
    for (genvar dim2 = 0; dim2 < DIM2; dim2++) begin
      assign internal[dim1][dim2+SHIFT] = in[dim1][dim2];
    end
  end

  for (genvar dim1 = 0; dim1 < DIM1; dim1++) begin
    for (genvar dim2 = 0; dim2 < DIM2; dim2++) begin
      always_ff @(negedge internal[dim1][dim2+SHIFT]) begin
        $display("%0b", internal[dim1][dim2+SHIFT]);
      end
    end
  end

endmodule
```

Use this command:

```bash
verilator test.sv --trace-fst --cc --exe --binary --main --top Test
```

It only crashes if `--trace-fst` is used. Without it, no error.

Here’s the error Verilator throws:

```
%Error: Internal Error: test.sv:9:40: ../V3FuncOpt.cpp:228: Inconsistent assignment
   9 |   logic [DIM1-1:0][DIM2+SHIFT-1:SHIFT] internal;
     |                                        ^~~~~~~~
```

## Version

Verilator 5.036 (2025-04-27)
Ubuntu 22.04
Always reproducible with `--trace-fst`
code_blocks:
module Test #(
  localparam int DIM1 = 4,
  localparam int DIM2 = 8,
  localparam int SHIFT = 2
) (
  input logic [DIM1-1:0][DIM2-1:0] in
);

  logic [DIM1-1:0][DIM2+SHIFT-1:SHIFT] internal;

  for (genvar dim1 = 0; dim1 < DIM1; dim1++) begin
    for (genvar dim2 = 0; dim2 < DIM2; dim2++) begin
      assign internal[dim1][dim2+SHIFT] = in[dim1][dim2];
    end
  end

  for (genvar dim1 = 0; dim1 < DIM1; dim1++) begin
    for (genvar dim2 = 0; dim2 < DIM2; dim2++) begin
      always_ff @(negedge internal[dim1][dim2+SHIFT]) begin
        $display("%0b", internal[dim1][dim2+SHIFT]);
      end
    end
  end

endmodule
verilator test.sv --trace-fst --cc --exe --binary --main --top Test
%Error: Internal Error: test.sv:9:40: ../V3FuncOpt.cpp:228: Inconsistent assignment
   9 |   logic [DIM1-1:0][DIM2+SHIFT-1:SHIFT] internal;
     |                                        ^~~~~~~~
